item.title: Save video Art Why European artists shifted their focus from power to peasants in the 16th century 5 minutes - Aeon
Save essay The ancient world The great libraries of Rome Passersby could wander at will into grand public libraries in imperial Rome. Could they trust what they found inside? Fabio Fernandes - Aeon
Save essay Nations and empires The Arab Kingdom Amid the chaos of the First World War, a new pan-Arab empire was proclaimed. It faltered, but its historical lessons remain Adam Mestyan - Aeon
Save video Technology and the self Why we should worry less about ‘sentient’ AIs and more about what we’re teaching them 16 minutes - Aeon
Save essay Psychiatry and psychotherapy Analysis for the people Group therapy promised to be both democratic and radical, but it failed to take hold. Has its time finally come? Jess Cotton - Aeon
Save video Biology To understand how an animal sees the world, start with the shape of its pupils 5 minutes - Aeon

Save essay Thinkers and theories Wittgenstein in the classroom The philosopher understood that learning – of a concept, of ourselves, of each other – is the undertaking of a whole life Calum Jacobs - Aeon
Save video Stories and literature Myths from Earth’s edge – what the Icelandic sagas reveal about Norse morality 57 minutes - Aeon

Save essay Political philosophy Liberalism against capitalism The work of John Rawls shows that liberal values of equality and freedom are fundamentally incompatible with capitalism Colin Bradley - Aeon
Save video Religion From God’s shoes to satellites in heaven – children weigh in on religion 8 minutes - Aeon

Save essay History of ideas Equality without compromise Liberal philosophy has clipped the wings of the egalitarian ideal. We should return to the bolder ideals of Iris Murdoch Christine Sypnowich - Aeon
Save video Gender When aggression is viewed as brilliance, it hurts women in science, and science itself 5 minutes - Aeon
Save essay Global history The polycrisis Is this the word we need to describe unprecedented convergences between ecological, political and economic strife? Ville Lähde - Aeon
Save video Physics A dreamy tribute to the music of Brian Eno, rendered in paint, soap and water 2 minutes - Aeon
Save essay Film and visual culture Exposed Slum photography was at the heart of progressive campaigns against urban poverty. And it was a weapon against poor people Sadie Levy Gale - Aeon
Save video Art Inside the unique creative space where ‘outsider’ artists find their form 14 minutes - Aeon
Save video Cities The rise and fall of Kowloon Walled City, Hong Kong’s infamous urban monolith 18 minutes - Aeon
Save video Meaning and the good life The world turns vivid, strange and philosophical for one plane crash survivor 16 minutes - Aeon
Save video Mathematics How a curious question about colouring maps changed mathematics forever 9 minutes - Aeon

Save essay Archaeology The secret life of Druids The Greeks and Romans portrayed these elusive priests as bogeymen who bathed in their victims’ blood. Who were they really? Miranda Aldhouse-Green - Aeon

Save essay Thinkers and theories Why seek self-realisation? In the face of climate crisis it might seem myopic but philosophers from Spinoza to Næss argue it is the only way forward Helen De Cruz - Aeon
Save essay Mood and emotion When grief doesn’t end Suffering the sudden death of a loved person leaves some survivors stuck in grief. Can they win their lives back – and how? Martin W Angler - Aeon
Save essay Palaeontology The dinosaurs didn’t rule When we think of changes in Earth’s history as changes of dynasty we miss out on understanding how life really works Riley Black - Aeon
Save essay Quantum theory We are not empty The concept of the atomic void is one of the most repeated mistakes in popular science. Molecules are packed with stuff Mario Barbatti - Aeon

Save essay Thinkers and theories When hope is a hindrance For Hannah Arendt, hope is a dangerous barrier to courageous action. In dark times, the miracle that saves the world is to act Samantha Rose Hill - Aeon
